title: CICS/KICKS: Transaction Processing
description: Map the transaction execution plane. Install and run CICS transactions on the TK5 lab.

steps.title: What is CICS?
steps.screen:
CICS — Customer Information Control System

CICS is a transaction processing monitor.
It manages:
- Short-lived, interactive transactions
- Shared resources (files, queues, programs)
- Terminal sessions (3270 and web)

KICKS = CICS-compatible subsystem for MVS/TK5
Runs CICS transactions without full CICS license.
steps.expected: Understand CICS as a transaction execution plane separate from TSO.
steps.hint: CICS transactions run under a SINGLE address space shared by ALL users — a key security boundary.

steps.title: Connect to CICS/KICKS
steps.screen:
VTAM USS Screen

APPLID ===> CICS2

Connecting to CICS2 application...

KICKS/MVS  Version 3.0
Copyright (C) 2009-2023 The KICKS Project

Clear screen or enter transaction ID.
steps.expected: Connect to the CICS/KICKS subsystem via VTAM APPLID.
steps.hint: Type CICS2 at the VTAM USS screen and press Enter to connect.

steps.title: Run CESN (Signon)
steps.screen:
KICKS TRANSACTION: CESN

SIGNON TO CICS

Userid   ===> CICSUSER
Password ===> ________

KICKS Security: RACF-integrated
All transactions run under the signed-on identity.
steps.expected: CICS signon binds a RACF identity to the CICS terminal session.
steps.hint: CESN = CICS signon transaction. Identity is bound here for all subsequent transactions.

steps.title: Run CEMT (Master Terminal)
steps.screen:
KICKS TRANSACTION: CEMT

STATUS SYSTEM
  Akpfreq(0)  Cicsstatus(Open)  Cmdprot(Enabled)
  Dtrpgm(Dfhdtrp)  Dtrtran(Cdtf)
  Maxtasks(10)  Mrobatch(1)
  Progautoexit(Dfhpgadx)
  Runaway(0)  Scandelay(0)

Command ===>
steps.expected: CEMT shows the CICS system status and configuration.
steps.hint: CEMT requires CICS SPECIAL authority — equivalent to RACF SPECIAL within CICS.

steps.title: Transaction Security Model
steps.screen:
CICS Transaction Security

Each transaction has:
- Transaction ID (4 chars)
- Transaction security key (1-64)
- User security key requirement

RACF CICS resource classes:
- TCICSTRN: transaction authorization
- GCICSTRN: group transaction auth

Gap: If RACF CICS classes are inactive,
ANY signed-on user can run ANY transaction.
steps.expected: Understand the CICS transaction authorization model.
steps.hint: Check if TCICSTRN class is active: SETROPTS LIST — if not, all transactions are open.

steps.title: Enumerate Transactions
steps.screen:
KICKS TRANSACTION: CEMT I TRAN

TRAN(CEDA)  STA(ENABLED)  USE(0)  PRI(1)
TRAN(CECI)  STA(ENABLED)  USE(0)  PRI(1)
TRAN(CEMT)  STA(ENABLED)  USE(0)  PRI(1)
TRAN(CESF)  STA(ENABLED)  USE(0)  PRI(1)
TRAN(CESN)  STA(ENABLED)  USE(0)  PRI(1)

CEDA = resource definition (admin)
CECI = command interpreter (dangerous)
steps.hint: CECI (Command Interpreter) allows arbitrary CICS API calls — highest risk transaction.

steps.title: CECI — Command Interpreter
steps.screen:
KICKS TRANSACTION: CECI

EXEC CICS COMMAND INTERPRETER

Enter command:
EXEC CICS WRITEQ TS QUEUE('ATTACK') FROM('PAYLOAD') LENGTH(7)

RESPONSE: NORMAL

CECI allows direct CICS API execution.
No JCL required. Immediate execution.
Runs under current user's identity.
steps.expected: CECI enables direct CICS API calls — a powerful attack tool.
steps.hint: CECI + CICS FILE WRITE = direct dataset modification without ISPF or JCL.

steps.title: Lab Complete
steps.screen:
CICS/KICKS Lab Complete

Key findings:
- CICS is a shared execution plane
- All users share ONE address space
- CECI = arbitrary API execution
- CEDA = resource definition (admin)
- TCICSTRN class controls transaction auth

Attack path:
1. Connect to CICS via VTAM
2. Signon with any valid RACF user
3. Run CECI if TCICSTRN inactive
4. Read/write datasets directly
steps.expected: Map the CICS transaction execution plane and its attack surface.
steps.hint: CICS is often the most exposed attack surface on a mainframe — it faces the network.
